channel.title: Context Engineering Daily - Research Papers
channel.description: Latest Research Papers news in Context Engineering

item.title: Toward Generalized Cross-Lingual Hateful Language Detection with Web-Scale Data and Ensemble LLM Annotations
item.description: arXiv:2604.09625v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We study whether large-scale unlabelled web data and LLM-based synthetic annotations can improve multilingual hate speech detection. Starting from texts crawled via OpenWebSearch.eu~(OWS) in four languages (English, German, Spanish, Vietnamese), we pu...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, model, LLM, study, fine-tuning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Claim2Vec: Embedding Fact-Check Claims for Multilingual Similarity and Clustering
item.description: arXiv:2604.09812v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recurrent claims present a major challenge for automated fact-checking systems designed to combat misinformation, especially in multilingual settings. While tasks such as claim matching and fact-checked claim retrieval aim to address this problem by l...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, analysis, model, embedding, alignment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Simulating Organized Group Behavior: New Framework, Benchmark, and Analysis
item.description: arXiv:2604.09874v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Simulating how organized groups (e.g., corporations) make decisions (e.g., responding to a competitor's move) is essential for understanding real-world dynamics and could benefit relevant applications (e.g., market prediction). In this paper, we forma...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> prompting, arxiv, analysis, prompt, platform | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Help Without Being Asked: A Deployed Proactive Agent System for On-Call Support with Continuous Self-Improvement
item.description: arXiv:2604.09579v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In large-scale cloud service platforms, thousands of customer tickets are generated daily and are typically handled through on-call dialogues. This high volume of on-call interactions imposes a substantial workload on human support analysts. Recent st...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, platform, model, large language model, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Factorizing formal contexts from closures of necessity operators
item.description: arXiv:2604.09582v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Factorizing datasets is an interesting process in a multitude of approaches, but many times it is not possible or efficient the computation of a factorization of the dataset. A method to obtain independent subcontexts of a formal context with Boolean ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, framework, study, paper, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Deliberative Alignment is Deep, but Uncertainty Remains: Inference time safety improvement in reasoning via attribution of unsafe behavior to base model
item.description: arXiv:2604.09665v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: While the wide adoption of refusal training in large language models (LLMs) has showcased improvements in model safety, recent works have highlighted shortcomings due to the shallow nature of these alignment methods. To this end, the work on Deliberat...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, model, LLM, alignment, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: A Comparative Theoretical Analysis of Entropy Control Methods in Reinforcement Learning
item.description: arXiv:2604.09676v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Reinforcement learning (RL) has become a key approach for enhancing reasoning in large language models (LLMs), yet scalable training is often hindered by the rapid collapse of policy entropy, which leads to premature convergence and performance satura...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, analysis, reasoning, model, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: ExecTune: Effective Steering of Black-Box LLMs with Guide Models
item.description: arXiv:2604.09741v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: For large language models deployed through black-box APIs, recurring inference costs often exceed one-time training costs. This motivates composed agentic systems that amortize expensive reasoning into reusable intermediate representations. We study a...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, analysis, API, model, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Efficient Matrix Implementation for Rotary Position Embedding
item.description: arXiv:2604.09742v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Rotary Position Embedding (RoPE) has become a core component of modern Transformer architectures across language, vision, and 3D domains. However, existing implementations rely on vector-level split and merge operations that introduce non-negligible c...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, attention, model, embedding, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Should We be Pedantic About Reasoning Errors in Machine Translation?
item.description: arXiv:2604.09890v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Across multiple language pairings (English $\to$ \{Spanish, French, German, Mandarin, Japanese, Urdu, Cantonese\}), we find reasoning errors in translation. To quantify how often these reasoning errors occur, we leverage an automated annotation protoc...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, model, experiment, RAG, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: LABBench2: An Improved Benchmark for AI Systems Performing Biology Research
item.description: arXiv:2604.09554v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Optimism for accelerating scientific discovery with AI continues to grow. Current applications of AI in scientific research range from training dedicated foundation models on scientific data to agentic autonomous hypothesis generation systems to AI-dr...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, tool, model, research, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Belief-State RWKV for Reinforcement Learning under Partial Observability
item.description: arXiv:2604.09671v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We propose a stronger formulation of RL on top of RWKV-style recurrent sequence models, in which the fixed-size recurrent state is explicitly interpreted as a belief state rather than an opaque hidden vector. Instead of conditioning policy and value o...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, model, experiment, vector, memory | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: GIANTS: Generative Insight Anticipation from Scientific Literature
item.description: arXiv:2604.09793v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Scientific breakthroughs often emerge from synthesizing prior ideas into novel contributions. While language models (LMs) show promise in scientific discovery, their ability to perform this targeted, literature-grounded synthesis remains underexplored...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, release, model, research, example | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Linear Programming for Multi-Criteria Assessment with Cardinal and Ordinal Data: A Pessimistic Virtual Gap Analysis
item.description: arXiv:2604.09555v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multi-criteria Analysis (MCA) is used to rank alternatives based on various criteria. Key MCA methods, such as Multiple Criteria Decision Making (MCDM) methods, estimate parameters for criteria to compute the performance of each alternative. Nonethele...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, arxiv, model, analysis | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Turing Test on Screen: A Benchmark for Mobile GUI Agent Humanization
item.description: arXiv:2604.09574v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The rise of autonomous GUI agents has triggered adversarial countermeasures from digital platforms, yet existing research prioritizes utility and robustness over the critical dimension of anti-detection. We argue that for agents to survive in human-ce...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, analysis, platform, model, research | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Explainable Planning for Hybrid Systems
item.description: arXiv:2604.09578v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The recent advancement in artificial intelligence (AI) technologies facilitates a paradigm shift toward automation. Autonomous systems are fully or partially replacing manually crafted ones. At the core of these systems is automated planning. With the...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> study, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Fairboard: a quantitative framework for equity assessment of healthcare models
item.description: arXiv:2604.09656v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Despite there now being more than 1,000 FDA-authorised AI medical devices, formal equity assessments -- whether model performance is uniform across patient subgroups -- are rare. Here, we evaluate the equity of 18 open-source brain tumour segmentation...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, analysis, release, model, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)
